title: Erica oreina
taxonomy: Erica oreina belongs to the family Ericaceae, commonly known as the heath family. This family consists of approximately 100 genera, with Erica oreina being a part of the Erica genus. The Erica genus is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as heaths, which are native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Africa, and Asia. The species Erica oreina is a specific type of heath that is endemic to certain regions. Further classification of Erica oreina can be determined through analysis of its botanical characteristics.

intro: Erica oreina, commonly known as the alpine heather, is a low-growing, flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. The health benefits of Erica oreina include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
